title: hibbertia semipilosa
intro: Hibbertia semipilosa, also known as the yellow buttons or yellow daisy, is a small, shrub-like plant native to Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The plant's leaves and stems contain compounds with potential health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, which may help to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and boost the immune system. Hibbertia semipilosa can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical creams and ointments to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
